Subject: Interim invoice acceptance & PO matching flow

Hi Marta —

Thanks again for the time this morning. Per our call, StellarNet will accept an interim invoice for the April usage run while the full vendor enrollment is completed in your portal. To keep payments flowing we want to align on the minimal PO and remittance metadata you need so AP can auto-match the invoice in your SAP/Oracle flow.

Quick summary of what we intend to provide now (and what we'll add to the portal):
- Interim Invoice (attached): one consolidated PDF covering April usage, reference: Interim_Invoice_000123.pdf
- PO tagging: please confirm the exact PO tag format you require (example: STELLAR-PO-000987) so we can embed it in invoice footer and PDF filename
- Line-level matching: if you need item-level quantities or SKU lines, tell us whether the matching key is SKU, PO line number, or a free-text description
- VAT/tax details: we'll attach Redwood's VAT registration and W9 where required (attached)

Can you confirm acceptance of the interim invoice on these terms and the PO tag format we should use? If StellarNet needs an AP contact or portal ticket created for our vendor record, let us know the reference and we'll follow up immediately.

I'll loop in Laura and Grace from our AP ops so they can provide the enrollment packet and any supporting evidence.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Interim_Invoice_000123.pdf (PDF)


From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@stellarnet.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Team <accounts-payable@stellarnet.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interim invoice acceptance & PO matching flow

Hi Ben —

Appreciate the clear breakdown. StellarNet can accept an interim invoice while vendor enrollment is in progress with the following conditions:
1) PO tag: must use the format STELLAR-PO-<7 digit> in the invoice filename and with a matching PDF footer. Example: STELLAR-PO-0123456
2) Matching key: we prefer PO line number when present, otherwise SKU. Free-text will require manual review and will delay payment.
3) Remit address: please include our remit code RN-7682 in the invoice body and on the PDF metadata.
4) VAT/tax evidence: vendor must upload VAT ID (EU) or W9 (US). We will accept the W9 PDF you attached for now.
5) Portal ticket: I created ticket SN-AP-4521 to track vendor record creation; please send us the completed onboarding packet and we will queue for processing (typical SLA 10 business days).

If you can rename the interim invoice to STELLAR-PO-0123456-Redwood-Interim.pdf and re-send, our AP ingestion will pick it up automatically.

Best,
Marta Ruiz
Finance Operations Manager
StellarNet

title: Post-call: traffic spec intake & POC commitments
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir@aureliahealth.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah_schmitt@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 02 May 2027 13:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: traffic spec intake & POC commitments

Hi Samir / Priya — thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so everyone is aligned:

Summary of what we heard:
- Goal: 3-week POC to validate inference latency and cost at peak traffic for Aurelia Health chat assistant (clinical triage use).
- Success criteria (customer-defined): 95th perc latency <= 350ms end-to-end for single-turn prompts; inference cost guidance target <$0.003/token average; no dropped requests during 30s bursts.

Traffic profile (what we captured on call):
- Baseline steady RPS: 8 rps (main clinic hours)
- Expected concurrency from UI: up to 10 concurrent sessions, each may generate 1-3 calls in short succession.
- Burst behavior: 80 rps for ~20-45s during scheduled daily events (reports from on-call team).
- Typical token lengths: prompts 40-120 tokens, generation 60-400 tokens (long tail to ~1,200 for diagnostic export).

Immediate asks we need to progress POC: 
1) Customer to confirm the 80 rps burst window(s) and provide 1-2 sample payloads (JSON) with header metadata (user_type, locale).
2) Customer to supply any latency SLOs beyond the 95th perc and any max token limits we should enforce.
3) Redwood to provide an ingestion readiness checklist, proposed canary dates, and mapping of owners for telemetry collection.

Owners & next steps (proposed):
- Ben Carter (Sales) — confirm POC commitment, finalize kickoff calendar invite (owner for scheduling).
- Hannah Schmitt (Solutions) — lead runtime config, batch/quantization suggestion + acceptance gate.
- Karthik Iyer (Infra/Eng) — run baseline benchmarks, validate KV-cache behavior for mult-turn flows.
- Samir / Priya — provide sample payloads and confirm window for 3-week trial (preferred start week of May 9).

Attachments: aurelia-traffic-sample.csv (traffic extract stub), poc-success-metrics.md (proposed acceptance criteria doc).

Proposed schedule:
- By Tue 5/4: Samir to send sample payloads and confirm start date.
- Wed 5/5: Redwood provides canary plan and preliminary config.
- Mon 5/9: Target POC start (3 weeks) if samples and approvals in place.

Samir — anything I missed or needs correction? If the dates work, I'll send invites and the runbook link.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0182

[attachment: aurelia-traffic-sample.csv (csv)]
[attachment: poc-success-metrics.md (md)]

From: Samir Patel <samir@aureliahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>
Date: Sun, 02 May 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call: traffic spec intake & POC commitments

Thanks Ben — this looks right. Quick clarifications from our side: 

- Start date: May 10 would be better for our ops calendar (Mon 5/10 - Fri 5/28 for a 3-week run).

Thanks again for the time today — good conversation and helpful context. Quick recap + asks so we can map to a Marketplace Private Offer (AWS) and give procurement a clean $/1M token unit to review.

- Procurement prefers to buy via AWS Marketplace Private Offer (PO) or partner-managed PO

Asks / data points we need to finalize a private-offer unit price:

2) Target budget range per month (ballpark is fine) so we can recommend a committed tier.
3) Procurement timing / target PO issuance date and the name on the PO.
4) Any constraints on billing metric (must be per-API-call vs per-1M-tokens, or must match AWS marketplace metering?)

Initial mapping guidance (for internal clarity): we normally map private-offer pricing to a $/1M tokens SKU so it cleanly shows on AP/finance side. See proposed examples in the attached pricing spreadsheet.

Next steps: if you can reply with the three bullets above by EOD Wednesday (5/4) we will: (a) draft a Private Offer pricing sheet, (b) prepare an AWS Marketplace-friendly metering metric, and (c) loop Raj (AWS partner rep) to start the PO flow.

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Raj Patel <raj.patel@aws-partner.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T13:50:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Priv offer PPU targeting & next steps — Zephyr Labs

Great — thanks for the numbers, Sarah. Those volumes and the budget band map cleanly to two unit-price scenarios we can put into a Private Offer. Below is a short worked example and the concrete next steps.

Worked examples (normalized to $/1M tokens):
- Scenario A (12-month commit, target mid-tier): $35 per 1M tokens
  * For 200M tokens/mo => 200 * $35 = $7,000 / mo (matches your low-end budget)
- Scenario B (month-to-month / no commit): $60 per 1M tokens
  * For 200M tokens/mo => 200 * $60 = $12,000 / mo (within your stated range)
- Upsell kicker (if they agree to a 24-month larger commit or Dedicated capacity): we can push to $22 per 1M tokens for a 12+ month high-commit tier at 500M+ tokens/mo.

Notes on mapping to AWS Private Offer (what we will do):
- We create a distinct SKU in the AWS Private Offer that bills per 1M tokens consumed. Metering will be implemented using the marketplace metering API so the PO invoice line shows the $/1M unit.
- We'll include a simple usage-to-cost example on the PO doc so finance can reconcile (I will put the math in the pricing sheet).
- Any marketplace fee considerations will be summarized in the private-offer doc (we'll show net vs gross to clarify finance impact).

Immediate next steps (owner in parentheses):
- I'll prepare a draft Private Offer pricing sheet with the two scenarios above and example invoices (Ben) — ETA: 5/4 EOD. (attaching proposed-private-offer-pricing.xlsx)
- Marissa, can you validate the PO wording (metering metric & SKU labels) so procurement has what they need? (Marissa)
- Karthik, can you prepare a quick note on runtime/quantization levers that Zephyr could use to reduce effective $/1M tokens by ~10–20% if they accept Dedicated or model-quantization suggestions? (Karthik)
- I'll loop Raj at AWS to confirm the Private Offer flow and expected timeline. (Ben)

If that plan looks good I'll send the pricing sheet and a suggested email for procurement to sign the Private Offer.

Thanks — Ben

Attachments: proposed-private-offer-pricing.xlsx

title: BrightFrame — Post-call commitments, stakeholders and timeline
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alyssa Morgan <alyssa@brightframe.com>, Tom Jenkins <t.jenkins@brightframe.com>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 01 May 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: BrightFrame — post-call summary & proposed commitments

Alyssa / Tom — thanks for the time today. Quick recap from our call + proposed next steps so we all have the same view. Please flag anything I missed.

Key pain points you shared
- Production hallucinations from current assistant when handling order exceptions (bad entity extraction + missing context).
- Token costs when batch-summarizing multi-message threads for case resolution.
- Need for predictable latency (<300ms p95) for the customer-facing escalation flow.

Goals you articulated
1) Replace the existing assistant on billing/support escalation with an inference-backed reranker + constrained generator to reduce incorrect resolutions.
2) Reduce per-ticket token cost by 30% via intelligent caching and model sizing in 90 days.
3) Reach a pilot SLA window (p95 latency <300ms) in controlled traffic during the pilot.

Proposed mutual action plan (high level) — see attached workbook for details
- Milestone A (Discovery + Eval): 2 weeks (May 3–May 16) — Redwood provides a scoped eval environment, sample prompts, and a metric baseline for accuracy and cost. Owners: Ben (Redwood), Alyssa (BrightFrame).
- Milestone B (Pilot): 6 weeks (May 17–Jun 30) — dedicated pilot capacity, instrumentation, and pre-production run. Owners: Grace (Redwood CS), Tom (BrightFrame Eng).
- Milestone C (Commercial + Procurement): 2–4 weeks after pilot — finalize SLA and procurement (SOW + T&Cs). Owners: Ben + BrightFrame procurement.

Decision process / stakeholders (as we understood)
- Product/Support: Tom Jenkins — technical sign-off (pilot success metrics).
- Customer Ops: Alyssa Morgan — business acceptance and exec sponsor.
- Procurement: procurement@brightframe.com — commercial and legal.

Open questions for BrightFrame before we lock dates
1) Can you confirm Tom has access to the sample dataset we discussed (anonymized thread exports)?
2) Procurement cadence — do you prefer a single contract or separate SOW + PO?
3) Are there any regional data residency constraints for the pilot?

Next steps (short)
- Alyssa to confirm dataset access and procurement contact (by Mon, May 3).
- Redwood to deliver the MPA workbook and pilot onboarding checklist (Ben → May 3).
- Grace to schedule a 30-min kickoff for the pilot scope with Tom (target week of May 10).

Attachments: BrightFrame_MutualActionPlan_v1.xlsx (MPA workbook), BrightFrame_SOW_outline.pdf (draft scope)

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0184

Cc: Tom Jenkins <t.jenkins@brightframe.com>, procurement@brightframe.com

- Procurement prefers a single SOW and then a PO; I'll loop our commercial lead (CC'd procurement alias) to start the template.
- No regional residency constraints for this pilot (US-only workloads are fine).

A couple of clarifications on milestones: can we shift Milestone B to start May 24 instead of May 17? Engineering is heads-down this week. If so, end date moves to Jul 7.

Also: can you confirm what success looks like for the reranker (precision/recall target or reduction in manual escalations)? We want a measurable acceptance criterion.

I'll confirm procurement contact by Monday.

—Alyssa
Alyssa Morgan
Head of Customer Ops | BrightFrame
alyssa@brightframe.com | +1 (212) 555-0199

To: Alyssa Morgan <alyssa@brightframe.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Jenkins <t.jenkins@brightframe.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 May 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BrightFrame — post-call summary & proposed commitments

Thanks Alyssa — shifting the pilot start to May 24 works for our onboarding calendar. Updated milestone dates below. Rishi, looping you for the engineering acceptance definition and to confirm the test harness.

Updated timeline (agreed)
- Milestone A (Discovery + Eval): May 3–May 21 (shortened to include data prep)
- Milestone B (Pilot): May 24–Jul 07 (6 weeks)
- Milestone C (Commercial): Jul 08–Jul 25

Proposed acceptance criteria (initial suggestion)
- Reranker: reduce incorrect top-1 resolution by 60% on the test set (measured against current assistant baseline).
- Cost: 30% net reduction in tokens per resolved ticket using caching + batching configuration.
- Latency: p95 < 300ms under the pilot’s expected concurrency (we’ll include a load profile in the workbook).

Rishi — can you confirm the expected artifacts we need to hand off to Tom for integration (SDK keys, inference endpoint, data contract)? If you prefer, we can create a short checklist in the MPA workbook.

title: Next steps + tri-zone demo rotation plan
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Nguyen <ava@novacorp.com>, Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@novacorp.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 2 May 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Next steps + tri-zone demo rotation plan

Hi Ava / Tom —

Thanks again for the call earlier — great to learn more about Novacorp's ingestion pipeline constraints and the privacy bar you need for MRQ workloads. Quick recap and a proposed scheduling approach so we can lock the technical deep dive: 

Recap (2 mins):
- Primary goal: validate inference latency under your 3k tokens/session traffic profile and talk through private deployment options.
- Stakeholders: Ava (Platform), Tom (Security), and a small engineering panel (~3).
- Critical constraints: 1) no non-US hosting for PII test dataset, 2) need an hour-long sandboxed demo with logs & KV caching checks.

Proposed next steps (scheduling):
Instead of trying to find one perfect time that works for everyone across US/EU/APAC, we usually run a rotating tri-zone cadence: one demo window each week that rotates regions so each stakeholder can join at a convenient local time over a 3-week cycle. Below are three concrete options — pick the one that fits your Q2 calendar and we’ll send calendar invites for the rotation.

Option A (starting week of May 10):
- Week A (US-friendly): Tue 10:00 AM PT / 1:00 PM ET (ideal for US engineering)
- Week B (EU-friendly): Tue 4:00 PM CET / 11:00 AM ET
- Week C (APAC-friendly): Tue 9:00 AM SGT / Mon 9:00 PM PT

Option B (compressed two-week rotation):
- Week 1: US morning 9:00 AM PT
- Week 2: EU afternoon 3:00 PM CET + simultaneous recording

If you prefer we can also run a single 90-minute deep-dive outside the rotation — say Wed May 12 at 2:00 PM ET — but that tends to make it harder for APAC stakeholders to attend live.

Attachments: I’ve attached a preliminary demo agenda and a mutual action plan to give the team visibility into who owns what.

Ask: Could you confirm (a) whether the rotation approach works for Novacorp, and (b) which option (A or B) and start week you prefer? Once you confirm we’ll route the invites and include the exact sandbox instructions.

Thanks — looking forward to continuing the conversation.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

Attachment stubs: demo-agenda.pdf (1 page), mutual-action-plan.xlsx (small workbook)

From: Ava Nguyen <ava@novacorp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <tom.ellis@novacorp.com>
Date: Mon, 3 May 2027 09:22:00 +01:00
Subject: Re: Next steps + tri-zone demo rotation plan

Thanks Ben — the recap looks right.

A few constraints from our side:
- Security (Tom) needs the sandbox to be hosted in our US account during the PII run — that’s non-negotiable.
- Our platform team can join live for US and EU windows, but APAC participation will likely be via recording unless we rotate into evening PT.
- Preferred start week: May 10 is fine. If you go with Option A, the EU-friendly window (Week B) should not start earlier than May 17 so our release freeze (May 12–16) doesn’t interfere.

So high-level preference: Option A, start May 10. Can you propose the exact dates for the three consecutive Tuesdays? Also please send an invite with the sandbox access steps and the agenda.

— Ava

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>

Subject: Next steps + tri-zone demo rotation plan

Hi Ava / Tom —

Thanks again for the call earlier — great to learn more about Novacorp's ingestion pipeline constraints and the privacy bar you need for MRQ workloads. Quick recap and a proposed scheduling approach so we can lock the technical deep dive: 

Recap (2 mins):
- Primary goal: validate inference latency under your 3k tokens/session traffic profile and talk through private deployment options.
- Stakeholders: Ava (Platform), Tom (Security), and a small engineering panel (~3).
- Critical constraints: 1) no non-US hosting for PII test dataset, 2) need an hour-long sandboxed demo with logs & KV caching checks.

Proposed next steps (scheduling):
Instead of trying to find one perfect time that works for everyone across US/EU/APAC, we usually run a rotating tri-zone cadence: one demo window each week that rotates regions so each stakeholder can join at a convenient local time over a 3-week cycle. Below are three concrete options — pick the one that fits your Q2 calendar and we’ll send calendar invites for the rotation.

Option A (starting week of May 10):
- Week A (US-friendly): Tue 10:00 AM PT / 1:00 PM ET (ideal for US engineering)
- Week B (EU-friendly): Tue 4:00 PM CET / 11:00 AM ET
- Week C (APAC-friendly): Tue 9:00 AM SGT / Mon 9:00 PM PT

Option B (compressed two-week rotation):
- Week 1: US morning 9:00 AM PT
- Week 2: EU afternoon 3:00 PM CET + simultaneous recording

If you prefer we can also run a single 90-minute deep-dive outside the rotation — say Wed May 12 at 2:00 PM ET — but that tends to make it harder for APAC stakeholders to attend live.

Attachments: I’ve attached a preliminary demo agenda and a mutual action plan to give the team visibility into who owns what.

Ask: Could you confirm (a) whether the rotation approach works for Novacorp, and (b) which option (A or B) and start week you prefer? Once you confirm we’ll route the invites and include the exact sandbox instructions.

Thanks — looking forward to continuing the conversation.

Best,
Ben Carter
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

So high-level preference: Option A, start May 10. Can you propose the exact dates for the three consecutive Tuesdays? Also please send an invite with the sandbox access steps and the agenda.

Hi Jordan,

We loved the short case film you did for Helix — would Redwood be interested in producing a 2-3 minute case film for Lumina Health? Our product team wants a short customer-facing piece spotlighting how Redwood helped us reduce inference cost and latency for our patient-facing assistant.

High level: 2 speakers (Chief Product Officer + Head of Ops), on-site, short B-roll (office + session shots), and a simple release to sign. Budget is modest; timeline target is early May if possible.

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>

Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-20T08:30:00-07:00
Subject: Draft talking points + interview guide for Lumina (first pass)

Hi Samira, Jordan —

Thanks for looping me in. Attached below is a short first-pass set of talking points and interview questions. I kept it tight so the final film stays under 3 minutes.

Key narrative arc (3 bullets):
- The challenge: scaling patient experience while keeping inference cost predictable
- The solution: how Redwood routing/quantization/caching cut costs and stabilized latency in production
- The outcome: measurable wins (percent cost reduction, latency p99 improvement, and improved CSAT)

Suggested openers (one-sentence soundbites):
- "We needed predictable latency at scale so our patient experience felt instant — Redwood helped make that reliable without breaking the budget."
- "The drop in inference cost let us prioritize product features that matter most to patients."

Interview questions (short):
1) What was the core problem you wanted to solve?
2) Why did you choose Redwood over building internally/other providers?
3) Can you share specific metrics or outcomes?
4) Any memorable customer-facing wins after the rollout?

Logistics notes: we typically record two takes per question, capture 15–30s B-roll of the product and office, and collect a signed release from each on-camera participant.

If you want to tweak tone (more technical vs more customer-facing), tell me and I’ll adapt the script.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Director, Product Marketing — Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

Attachment: (talking points included inline).

- Equipment: mirrorless camera, shotgun mic + lavs, portable LED kit, backup Zoom H6 recorder. We bring a remote-record backup (USB interface + laptop) in case there are AV issues.

title: VPC schematic validation + SLA matrix review
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@edgeworks.com>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-05-02T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: VPC schematic validation + SLA matrix review

Hi Samir —

Attached is our latest VPC schematic (v3) and a draft SLA matrix that maps service boundaries to SLO targets. Key asks:

- Can your team confirm that the proposed NAT/egress model and IP ranges in the schematic are compatible with EdgeWorks' transit fabric? (see diagram pages 2–3)
- For the control plane peering: do you support an mTLS-protected BGP session over the private L2/L3 link, or do you prefer an L3 GRE+IPsec tunnel?
- Can you confirm whether EdgeWorks can provide an allowlist for the control-plane management IPs and whether there are per-tenant ACL requirements?

Attachments: vpc_schematic_v3.pdf, sla_matrix_proposal.xlsx

title: SIG / SOC2 evidence: export retention boundaries and jurisdictional options
preview:
From: Mark Rivera <mark.rivera@solarahealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: privacy@solarahealth.com
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 09:08:00 -0700
Subject: Admin activity export — jurisdiction / retention question

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday. We’re working through the SIG and SOC2 evidence checklist and hit a question about admin activity / audit-log exports. Two quick items we need to confirm before we mark the control as answered: 

1) Does the admin-activity export include per-event timezone-normalized timestamps (UTC) and the original local timezone? We need both for our internal reconciliation.
2) Can you provide a short note on jurisdictional handling — specifically if a customer requests an export that includes activity for users in the EU, can that data be exported out of-region, or is there an on-region packaged export option? Our privacy team needs the wording for the SIG response.

If you can point to a sample manifest or a short statement we can include in the questionnaire that’d be ideal. Thanks — Mark

(attachment: sig-questionnaire.pdf - 320 KB)


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mark Rivera <mark.rivera@solarahealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 10:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Admin activity export — jurisdiction / retention question

Hi Mark — great to hear from you and glad the demo was useful. Short answers up front, then details below.

1) Yes — exports include UTC-normalized timestamps and we retain the original timezone string on each event.
2) We support both cross-region exports and a geofenced packaged export. By default hosted exports are generated in the region where the tenant is provisioned; for EU tenants we can produce an on-region sealed bundle.

I’m looping in Kimberly from Legal to provide suggested SIG language and any required caveats. Neha on infra is cc’d for the export manifest sample and the small CSV with field-level examples.

Next steps: Kimberly will draft a short paragraph you can paste into the SIG; Neha will attach an export manifest sample. Let me know if you need the export delivered to an SFTP or a signed Drive link.

Best,
Ava

--
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwoodinference.com
(attachment referenced: export_manifest_sample.csv - stub)


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mark Rivera <mark.rivera@solarahealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Admin activity export — jurisdiction / retention question

Mark — provided below is a suggested SIG / CAIQ response you can paste into the questionnaire. It’s intentionally short and prescriptive. If Solara needs specific contract language we can follow up with legal.

Suggested respondent text (SIG / CAIQ):
"Redwood Inference logs administrative and operator activity at the per-event level, including user identity, action type, resource targeted, and a timezone-normalized timestamp (UTC). The original local timezone string is stored alongside each event for reconciliation and forensic purposes. Export options include: (a) cross-region encrypted export delivered to customer-specified storage endpoints; and (b) an on-region sealed export bundle for tenants provisioned in-region (e.g., EU region) to support data residency requirements. Exports are authenticated and integrity-protected; access to export delivery is logged and retained per the customer's retention policy."

Retention note: our hosted default retention for admin activity is 13 months, with configurable shorter/longer retention windows available on Dedicated and Private deployments. Exports are generated as immutable, signed bundles and include a manifest describing event counts, time window, and checksum.

If you want, I can add a short clause about exceptional legal requests (e.g., subpoenas) — we typically refer to the contract/legal team for those scenarios.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Privacy & Compliance | Redwood Inference


From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Mark Rivera <mark.rivera@solarahealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 02 May 2027 15:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Admin activity export — jurisdiction / retention question

Hi Mark — attaching a tiny sample manifest and an example CSV row to show the fields we include. Notes below.

Attachment stubs:
- export_manifest_sample.csv (attachment: export_manifest_sample.csv - 4 KB)
- field_legend.txt (attachment: field_legend.txt - 1 KB)

Key technical points: 
- Fields included per event: event_id, actor_id (internal id), actor_email (if available), action, resource_type, resource_id, original_timestamp, utc_timestamp, timezone, request_id, result_status, kv_cache_key (nullable).
- Timestamps: utc_timestamp is ISO8601 with Z; original_timestamp holds the inbound timestamp recorded by the origin service and includes the timezone string.
- Export packaging: we produce gzipped NDJSON (line-delimited JSON) for large windows; for smaller windows we generate CSV. Every bundle includes: manifest.json (summary line counts/sha256), bundle.sig (detached signature), and README.md with redaction notes.
- Jurisdictional handling: for tenants marked as EU-resident we default exports to the eu-central region. Cross-region exports are available only after customer explicit consent captured in the export request form and an export-level consent flag is recorded on the manifest.
- Retention controls: retention is enforced at the storage layer; on Dedicated/Private deployments administrators can request retention changes via the Console or API. For hosted tenants we require a signed request for retention rates outside the default SLA.

Happy to walk through specifics on a quick call if your privacy team prefers — we can run a sample export and demonstrate the manifest / signature validation.

-neha

Neha Kapoor
SRE, Inference Platform | Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com

title: Multi-cloud listing & packaging — initial offer bundle and region gating
preview:
From: Hannah Lee <hannah@aws-partners.amazon.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: AWS co-sell / listing — questions on SKUs and regional packaging

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the intro at the partner summit — great to see Redwood's momentum. Our marketplace onboarding team asked me to confirm a few details before we schedule the formal listing intake:

- Which offer SKUs do you plan to list on AWS Marketplace at launch? (hosted API tiers, dedicated/reserved, private/VPC?)
- Do you want per-region SKUs (US/EU/APAC) or a global SKU with region gating in the offer?
- Any packaging constraints (metered vs. subscription vs. private offers) we should be aware of for co-sell qualification?

If you already have a proposed matrix, can you share it? If not, happy to run through options on a quick 30 min call next week. Also flagging the standard co-sell checklist: SOC 2, a public product page, and a partner-engaged case study or reference for the first 90 days.

Best,
Hannah
Partner Programs — AWS Marketplace

---
Attachment: aws-marketplace-checklist.pdf (stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 26 Apr 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: AWS co-sell / listing — questions on SKUs and regional packaging

Folks — forwarding Hannah's note. Can you help map a defensible initial set of offers for AWS and call out differences for GCP/Azure? High level thinking below. I've attached a working matrix (redwood-offer-matrix-v1.xlsx).

Proposed initial SKU constructs (draft):
- hosted-api-standard (metered token consumption, monthly subscription fallback)
- hosted-api-pro (higher RPS TTL + SLOs, subscription + metered)
- dedicated-commit-1x (1x reserved GPU pool; subscription annual)
- dedicated-commit-4x (4x pool, enterprise pricing)
- private-vpc-essentials (VPC connector + private control plane; enterprise-only offer)

Regional guidance (draft):

title: Cohort onboarding: credit bundling & tax treatment
preview:
From: Dana Lee <dana@seedlab.vc>
To: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 28 Apr 2027 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Cohort onboarding — combining stipend + promo question

Hi Olga & Ben,

Thanks again for the demo last week — SeedLab Ventures is excited about the cohort slot for our Q3 batch. Two quick questions from our finance team about the onboarding stipend you mentioned: can the cohort onboarding credits be combined with the new promotional trial (25k free tokens) or do they need to be accounted separately for invoicing/tax purposes?

Specifically:
- We typically need one line-item that maps to our accounting code (so an aggregated credit vs two separate credits matters).
- If the credits are combinable, will Redwood issue one consolidated invoice/statement that shows the applied credits and the residual billing?
- Are there any implications for 1099s or VAT reporting we should be aware of for US-based startups in the cohort?

If there are standard packaging options, happy to pick the simplest that avoids extra bookkeeping on our side. I’ve attached a very short outline of how our cohort funds flow.

Thanks — can we get an answer by EOD Friday? We’re trying to lock the grant terms with cohort ops.

Best,
Dana
Partner Programs | SeedLab Ventures
Dana Lee
Attachment: SeedLab_Onboarding_Flow.pdf (see doc)


From: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
To: Dana Lee <dana@seedlab.vc>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Cohort onboarding — combining stipend + promo question

Hi Dana — great to hear SeedLab is moving forward. Short answer: yes, we have two practical packaging paths; choice affects invoicing and tax treatment. Details below so your finance team can pick.

Option A — Consolidated credit (recommended for minimal bookkeeping):
- We combine the cohort stipend and the promotional trial into a single onboarding credit package.
- Redwood will issue one consolidated credit statement showing total credits applied and one invoice line for any post-credit consumption.
- Accounting/tax: for US companies this is treated as a billing credit applied against services; no separate 1099 from Redwood to the startup — Redwood records the credit as contra-revenue.

Option B — Segregated credits (preferred if you need grant-level tracking):
- We keep the cohort stipend and promo trial as separate ledger entries. Each will be represented on your billing statement as distinct line items.
- This makes it easier for SeedLab to report the stipend as a grant if you need to allocate to your portfolio companies individually.
- Accounting/tax: could require additional documentation for grants; we can produce a supplemental CSV showing credit IDs and allocations.

Things to note:
- If any portion of the stipend is converted to an invoiceable discount (e.g., top-up beyond free tokens), that will appear as normal billed usage.
- For VAT in EU cases we’ll need your VAT ID; we’ll apply local rules. For US 1099s, startups typically don’t receive a 1099 from Redwood for credits applied to their account.

Next steps:
- If Option A works, reply and we’ll draft the consolidated credit statement and push it into the cohort enrollment flow (no SSO/legal changes).
- If Option B works, please attach the allocation list (company names + accounting codes) and we’ll include the supplemental CSV with the first invoice.

I’ve attached a short one-page MOP showing how credits appear on invoices and a sample CSV export.

Olga
Olga Petrov
Senior Sales Manager, Enterprise & Programs | Redwood Inference
Attachments: SeedLab_Onboarding_MOP_v1.pdf, redit_contract_summary.xlsx


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 29 Apr 2027 11:30:00 -0700
Subject: INTERNAL: SeedLab credit nuance — approval request

Olga / Lauren — quick internal note (cc’d Dana on the external thread). SeedLab is asking for flexibility to present a single line-item to their accounting team but also wants grant-level visibility for portfolio allocations. My recommendation: offer Option A by default (consolidated) and include an optional supplemental CSV export for their cohort admins (no additional discount). That avoids creating a carveout on our books while giving them the reporting they want.

Two asks for Lauren:
1) Legal/Tax: confirm there’s no P&L exposure if we produce a supplemental CSV that ties credits to portfolio startups (this is just reporting, not a transfer of funds).
2) Pricing: approve standard cohort promo stacking (cohort stipend + 25k trial) as a packaged offering under DLV-9234. If approved I’ll mark this as a standard option in the Program Guide.

If either of you prefer to treat this as a bespoke credit split (and charge an ops fee), say so — we can propose a nominal $500 setup fee.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Launch Programs | Redwood Inference


From: Dana Lee <dana@seedlab.vc>
To: Olga Petrov <olga@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 02 May 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Cohort onboarding — combining stipend + promo question

Thanks — appreciate the clear options. SeedLab elects Option B (segregated credits) because we need ledger-level mapping for our grant distributions. We will provide the allocation spreadsheet by EOD Monday (company name + cohort seat ID + accounting code).

One quick follow-ups:
- Can Redwood generate the supplemental CSV as a signed, timestamped artifact for our audit trail? If not, we’ll accept the standard CSV but will need the invoice + CSV together to satisfy auditors.
- Timeline: once we send the allocation list, how long until the credits appear on each portfolio company account? We’re trying to enable a 7-day onboarding window.

If you can send a DocuSign for the cohort MOP amendment (small addendum), I can get our legal to sign this week.

Thanks — looking forward to getting the cohort live.

Best,
Dana
Partner Programs | SeedLab Ventures

> - This makes it easier for SeedLab to report the stipend as a grant if you need to allocate to your portfolio companies individually.
> - Accounting/tax: could require additional documentation for grants; we can produce a supplemental CSV showing credit IDs and allocations.
> 
> Things to note:
> - If any portion of the stipend is converted to an invoiceable discount (e.g., top-up beyond free tokens), that will appear as normal billed usage.
> - For VAT in EU cases we’ll need your VAT ID; we’ll apply local rules. For US 1099s, startups typically don’t receive a 1099 from Redwood for credits applied to their account.
> 
> Olga
> Olga Petrov
> Senior Sales Manager, Enterprise & Programs | Redwood Inference
> Attachments: SeedLab_Onboarding_MOP_v1.pdf, redit_contract_summary.xlsx

title: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>, ops@cloudops.dev
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 20 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Hi Rajesh / CloudOps team,

Following our discovery last week, I'm proposing a lightweight, auditable approach for version pinning and scheduled maintenance for your private VPC deployment of Redwood. Goal is to minimize customer-side network change windows while giving us deterministic rollback and audit trails for compliance.

Attachments: vpc_pin_matrix_v2.xlsx (matrix with proposed pins & staging order)

Quick summary of asks:
- Please confirm blackout windows / preferred maintenance windows for April->June.
- Confirm whether GreenBank can accept a 2 hour window with a 30-minute canary & 90-minute progressive shift.
- Any firewall/ACL freeze windows or change control restrictions we need to know about (NOC/Change board times).

High-level proposal (attached matrix):
- Canary cluster: pin to 2.15.x (latest minor) for 24–48h validation.
- Production pin: initialize to 2.14.1 (current tested baseline) and schedule a controlled bump to 2.15.1 only after green canary signals.
- Rollback: automatic traffic cutback to pinned 2.14.1 and KV cache restore; database/data-plane schema no-ops.

I'll coordinate runbooks, health checks, and a final ASM (approval) doc once we lock a window. Jordan / Dev — looping you for canary plan and monitoring hooks.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
mobile: +1-415-555-0129

---



From: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, ops@cloudops.dev
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 21 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Camila — thanks for the summary. A few items from our side: 

- Preferred maintenance window: Tue/Wed 02:00–04:00 UTC (local banking hours unaffected). We can do a Saturday window if an emergency full-restart is needed, but prefer weekday overnight for monitoring staff onsite.
- Change freeze: our infra change freeze for transaction systems is 00:00–06:00 UTC Sundays; we cannot touch ACLs in that period. That said, ACLs can be scheduled during business days with 48h notice.
- Approvals: I will submit a Change Request to CAB; please provide the canary checklist and rollback steps to attach.

We'll review the attached matrix and give a firm OK by EOD Friday.

Rajesh
Infra Ops | GreenBank Financial

On Tue, Apr 20, 2027 at 09:12 AM Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Following our discovery last week, I'm proposing a lightweight, auditable approach...



From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>
Date: Thu, 22 Apr 2027 10:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Camila / Jordan — quick internal note while Rajesh reviews:

I've sketched a canary plan to attach to the CAB packet. Key points:
- Canary scope = 5% production traffic for 2.15.x on isolated pool (no access to PII indices).
- Telemetry: latency p95, token error-rate, auth failures, and packet-drop from the VPC egress counters.
- Fallback automation: immediate route shift (traffic-proxy flag) + orchestration to rehydrate KV cache from snapshot.
- Manual gate at 30 minutes with explicit "proceed" from on-call SRE.

Attachment: canary-checklist.md (checklist with probe endpoints and play-by-play)

Also: we need Jordan to confirm we can perform the rota shift without touching customer-managed ACLs (we should rely on our egress NAT/ENI config). If ACL changes are required, CAB will need 72h notice.

-Dev



From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>, ops@cloudops.dev
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 25 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

Thanks for the quick turn, Rajesh. Notes after internal alignment: 

1) Pinning decisions:
- Production: we will pin production clusters to 2.14.1 for the immediate baseline.
- Canary: target 2.15.1 for canary pool. If canary passes for 24h (no regressions), a controlled roll to 2.15.1 for production will be scheduled but must be explicitly approved by GreenBank CAB.

2) Rollback mechanics (brief):
- If canary or early production signals breach thresholds (p95 latency +50ms or token errors >0.5%), we execute traffic proxy rollback to 2.14.1.
- KV restore: automated snapshot taken pre-cut. Snapshot retention TTL 48h for rollback window.
- Post-rollback verification plan: smoke write/read on <sanitized-test-queue> + end-to-end test run.

3) Network constraints:
- We will avoid customer ACL edits. Traffic shifts will be done at our egress layer (no ACL change needed). If ACLs are unavoidable, we will request 72h CAB approval.

4) Operational commitments:
- Monitoring hooks: Dev will expose the canary metrics dashboard and set three alert thresholds.
- DR rehearsals: Tessa to schedule a dry-run of rollback without traffic impact (dry-run uses staging tenants).

Next steps: Rajesh to confirm the CAB timeline and whether the Tuesday 02:00–04:00 UTC window is acceptable for the first canary attempt on 2027-04-30. After CAB ack, we'll send the final maintenance-runbook bundle (incl. canary-checklist.md, vpc_pin_matrix_v2.xlsx).

Camila



From: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 Apr 2027 15:06:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades

All — I've blocked the dry-run on 2027-04-28 18:00 UTC and the live canary run on 2027-04-30 02:00–04:00 UTC in the shared calendar (scheduled-windows.ics attached).

Notes on verification & alerts:
- Post-cut validation checklist (30min & 120min): smoke API, embedding-rate, tokenization validation, and a sanity run against the synthetic transaction harness.
- Alerting: jump to P0 if auth failures > 0.2% for 10m or p95 increase > 100ms for 5m.
- On-call: Naomi will be primary on the first canary window; Jordan handles rollback orchestration.

I'll run the dry-run with a simulated traffic switch (no customer traffic) and capture the runbook timing to ensure the real window fits in 2 hours.

Thanks — Tessa



From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Rajesh Patel <rajesh.patel@greenbank.com>, ops@cloudops.dev
Cc: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 30 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pinning calendar + fallback playbook for VPC/private upgrades — FINAL WINDOW

Team — final confirmation: GreenBank CAB approved the Tuesday overnight window. We are scheduling the first canary attempt on 2027-04-30 02:00–04:00 UTC. The attached bundle is the approved runbook. Please ACK back to this thread if you are on-call/monitoring during the window.

Attachments: vpc_pin_matrix_v2.xlsx, canary-checklist.md, scheduled-windows.ics

Summary of important bullets (final):
- Production baseline pinned at 2.14.1; canary pool to 2.15.1.
- Canary = 5% traffic, 30-minute manual gate, 90-minute progressive shift if green.
- Rollback: automated proxy shift + KV snapshot rehydrate; post-rollback smoke tests mandatory.
- Network: no customer ACL edits required; we will use egress routing. If any forced ACL change becomes necessary, we pause and re-schedule.
- On-call: Naomi primary, Jordan rollback lead, Tessa dry-run owner.

Please reply with a one-line ACK (I am on-call / I can monitor / I will be standby). If no objections by 23:59 UTC today, we'll treat silence as implicit ack.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

title: Request: short-stop demo subsidy while procurement finishes
preview:
From: Jorge Carvajal <jorge.carvajal@brightwell.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procure@brightwell.ai
Date: 2027-04-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick ask — procurement delaying PO, can you patch the demo?

Hi Ava,

We're excited about spinning up Redwood for a 2-week pilot, but our procurement team is taking longer than expected to finish the PO and security checklist. We have stakeholders ready to validate the integration this week and don't want momentum to stall.

Is there any way you can temporarily extend our trial window or provide a small run of credits so our engineers can finish the end-to-end tests while legal/finance nails down the PO? Even a 7–10 day buffer would keep the demo schedule intact. I attached the onboarding checklist so you can see the scope.

Thanks — happy to jump on a quick call if that helps.

Best,
Jorge
Head of Platform Partnerships
Brightwell Analytics
---
Attachment: Brightwell_Onboarding_Checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet)


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jorge Carvajal <jorge.carvajal@brightwell.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2027-04-28T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick ask — procurement delaying PO, can you patch the demo?

Hi Jorge — thanks for the heads up and the checklist. Appreciate the context.

Short answer: yes, we can help keep the pilot live. I can authorize a 10-day sandbox uplift (temporary credits) so your engineers can complete integration tests while procurement finalizes the PO.

Quick notes:
- The uplift is time-boxed and will expire automatically after 10 days.
